content: Fake mixup slop character:
He has swap mixups and feint mixups. But even with his neutral swaps being limited, his feint is also limited.
After a feint Todo can no longer feint, becoming generic and predictable.
overview.author: Nickolas4F

content: Swaps are  a mediocre option overall
(when the players get mechanical skill, after 20 ELO 💀 basically)
If you want to use them keep them as:

items: A) feints for mixups (M1 reset with swift kick is green, so it's a mixup only)
C) feints for extending out of elbow drop and BlackFlash

D) annoying your opponent: if they don't have a side dash ready and pray they don't track you as you track them 😌 meaning you need to already be better at the game then your opponent to profit of your swap mixups
E) fake anti zoning: You can swap around with ryus, hanamis, kuros to mess up their aim. You could have just dodged though. Also you can use them to swap with mechamaru's puppets

content: Other than that you're an M1 merchant
You don't have bait:
3 m1s swift kick, throw them up, block, pebble throw, uppercut/elbow drop, block for evasive, downslsm hrhshfjd

strategy.content: You can combo into any skill from any skill:

items: B) 4, 3, 1, 2 (if they don't bounce on four your combo will fail

strategy.content: Your basic combo path to bait rc:

content: 3 m1s swift kick, aim upwards so they have more ragdol time,
you can then hold block, expecting an evasive and if they don't (and are far away) you can go for pebble throw.
And then uppercut/elbow drop.
However if they don't have used their evasive you need to be wary so they don't punish you,
(since your combo is punishable rn)

strategy.content: Or you can play for mixups:

items: A) (unreliable against players who aren't bad): 3 m1s, 1, feint, M1 reset (green), m1s, brute force/elbow drop depending if they have a counter or not)

B) DOWNSLAM, pebble throw, side dash M1 around their block. You have m1 priority there (pretty sure), you can m1 and then block and then side dash M1. However you force an M1 trade, they can still M1 you or win the trade.

content: If you want true (lame though) DMG: 3 m1s, elbow drop, feint, swift kick, aim up and go for a downalam,
being wary of evasive (basically don't do the downslam, to make them evasive, thinking they get to punish you)
strategy.author: Nickolas4F

content: Again he is just a better M1 merchant than you. Due to 1r3 being again 40 free DMG if you don't evasive, better bait.
Nothing to be said, he has better mobility than you, the only thing is that yuji has the hp disadvantage
so he can't tank as much to fight your evasive bait. You basically out DMG and tank him.
But yuji has a counter, making brute force locked behind rc
You may swap and dodge a 1+2.. which is pointless.. you could have just blocked,
yeah you're losing though, he has a counter so brute force is locked behind Ragdol cancel.
He has better safe DMG than you, so he beats you to that
Unlike with choso, you don't deny shit.
Stockpile denies M1 trades, soul fire is ranged.
he beats you just because of stockpile, he can afford do smth' stupid for you to punish him,
just so he can go again and deny your M1 trade
Yeah... Gg no re. Todo swap can change the location of the orb, to deny that.
But most good choso's press R and detonate right after.
Not letting your swap to matter. Or they go for a Frontdash with it
Schizophrenia simulator, y'all just goofing around with swaps
Better gap closers, better mixups, you're making him look actually good.
Rika smash, RIKA haymaker, second wind will catch your dumb swap, outburst if you go in after a swap.
You could swap with his puppets to deny some of his neutral,
and I think swap his puppets with him (I need to check that out) but you still lose to true DMG evasive bait, neutral.
If he does R1 in an M1 trade, i don't think you should swap
Really.... You can get some chip out off 4r1, but nothing really else.
You can chip him down, but your swaps are entirely useless, he can reach double the distance,
you won't ever be able to swap around naoya.
You have to skill diff in M1 trades to win.
But youre getting slaughtered (if you ever swap, he can double dash to you before the swap happens and hit you)
Since nanami's neutral is more telegraphed.
You could work around his 1 and 3 by swapping and turning a random direction.
But I do think swap is slower than his moves. So you will get your legs broken.
He has overall better DMG with a worse bait
RUSHDOWN Vs zoner. You have zero chances,
you could stall with your swap to not get hit by her neutral (maybe) but her Frontdash is there if you start swapping.
Also she can just block one swap and kill you
Swap and get hit by second helping, neutral mogged,
he is also a zoner who can just hold S and profit, not taking part in your swaps.
He gets 40 Dmg out of an M1 and your evasive, you're both M1 merchants, he is just better in every way.
2rr, 4r, 3, Garuda stab is a better block Hitter than elbow drop,
yuki and also catch a swap with rebound if you spam it mindlessly
Counters locking brute force out of RC, his R special make you lose because you need to M1 trade
Unlike others you don't have Mele iframes on any skill, so his 1 and 2 are going to kill you instantly
If you try to swap up close,
she gets to do air 2 or 3 and put you in a combo, nothing to be said,
you can't spam swaps either since she forces block
He can pull you in and he has his 1 for your M1 trades.

content.content: Switcher's threat with his moves is GONE

content: now that pebble throw isn't a 45 true DMG extender and only 16.
if you get hit by pebble throw his option is the fourth M1 or elbow drop (swift kick too)
but after that he is really punishable with NO FEINT BTW. so if you get hit by an uppercut?
You can evasive on any of his skills and fully punish them, if you get hit by 4, then he has ONLY his last M1.
Where you can evasive and punish the DOWNSLAM or any other skill.
Best thing Todo can do is delay where he is getting punished.
Or drop the combo after elbow drop (he won't have swaps either way)
If you feel threatened from that well...🫩... Anywyas after doing uppercut into swaps,
he can only go for wakeup elbow drop or swift kick.
For swift kick you want to evasive and hold block (if you're bad at the game) or you want to evasive M1 into him,
so even if he feints it doesn't matter.
If they uppercut into elbow drop and you know they have their swap ready.
Don't bother with your evasive. Best case it's 30 DMG for Todo where he can't extend or it's a punishable extension.
Now there are 2 cases

items: A) he uppercuts feints and you don't evasive. Now he either resets to neutral or does another skill (punishable Trully)
B) he lands elbow drop, feints into swift kick, and yet again he can't extend (no swap) doing mediocre DMG which you will heal

content: The moment you start tracking you stop being a noob and swaps become 100000000x worse
The swap has 3ranges of use: long rage where it's Blockable,
mid range where it isn't Blockable but todo gets almost zero benefit from it,
close range (side dash range) where IF YOU KEEP YOUR SIDE DSSH, swaps are a death sentence for Todo.
Since your side dash M1 is faster than swap or swap feint.

content.content: For mid and long range swaps

content: don't bother, todo is just doing bs and swelling
